claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: NumberField with Intl currency formatting and one canonical hidden form control
semantics.naming: A persistent visible label identifies both the amount and its explicit currency context.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.
semantics.refTarget: HTMLDivElement root; inputRef reaches the editable HTMLInputElement

interaction.keyboard: Locale-aware currency editing and numeric step commands inherit the NumberField contract.
interaction.pointer: Stepper and optional scrub controls inherit the NumberField boundary and cancellation behavior.
interaction.touch: Coarse-pointer controls preserve at least a 44 CSS pixel preferred target.
interaction.speech: The visible amount label and localized control names remain available for voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: Every value-changing affordance is a real focusable input or button.

formBehavior: The hidden successful value is a canonical major-unit number without symbol or localized separators; the currency code remains explicit component configuration and must be submitted separately when the receiving workflow requires it.

states.synchronization: Visible localized currency text, numeric state, validation, step controls, and hidden major-unit serialization share one NumberField engine.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Uses semantic tokens and non-color state cues.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, visible borders, and system-color focus indicators.
preferences.reducedMotion: No essential information or interaction depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: Currency text scrolls within the input while labels and messages wrap without clipping.

internationalization.imePolicy: The editable input retains NumberField composition handling and does not commit partial IME text.
internationalization.serialization: Intl localizes the visible currency text; the JavaScript and form value remain canonical major units and the required currency code remains explicit.

consumerResponsibilities: Supply the correct currency code from trusted workflow data and submit that code separately if the backend amount contract requires it.
Use allowNegative only when the domain supports credits, refunds, or signed balances.
Choose an explicit precision when business rules differ from Intl currency minor-unit defaults.
Do not infer the canonical amount from the localized visible string.
limitations: The component does not perform exchange, rounding-ledger, taxation, or currency conversion logic.
Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, package parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
